# split_h_2p_demo velocity-smoothness fix — Audit Protocol audit-1

**Branch/commit audited:** `claude/persp-c-horiz-review-ntqlca` @ `61580f1` (the 8.8 fixed-point forward-velocity fix for the `-DROTATE` build of the previously audited-CLEAN `split_h_2p_demo` rail: `gen_assets.py` + regenerated `move64.bin` + `main.asm` movement block + handoff-brief Task 1).
**Role:** independent AUDIT-1 — I did not build this increment. All evidence regenerated by me from a fresh materialization (`dryrun_split.sh /tmp/kit_audit_vel`), all measurements on the cycle-accurate emulator via MesenRunner. `/home/user/SuperForge` untouched (`git status` clean, HEAD = 61580f1); the pre-commit comparison ROM and asset regeneration ran in scratch copies outside both trees.
**Method:** diff-scope check → hand verification of the two's-complement 8.8 decomposition (incl. the negative case v = −362 = $FE96) → independent LUT parse + delete-and-regenerate byte-compare → fresh build of all 7 ROMs → 18-test suite + 56-test regression + four gates → 104 stepped frames on the new rotate build verified frame-exact against my own independent model → 64 stepped frames on a pre-commit rotate build (rebuilt from `9c22b79`) measuring the old defect → boot-to-frame-116 whole-trajectory replay → byte-compare of all five non-ROTATE ROMs pre/post commit → width read of the new block → hardware-model review of the handoff Task-1 design against the emulator source.

## VERDICT: CLEAN (ship)

The fix does exactly what it claims, verified at instruction-model exactness on the emulator. Over 104 stepped frames (two runs) every single frame's POS1/POS2 delta, heading, divider, and all four fraction accumulators match my independent floor/mod model of the 8.8 decomposition — and a full replay from boot (F=0 via the coldstart WRAM clear) reproduces the emulator's frame-116 state word-for-word on all 12 state words, which simultaneously proves the accumulators start defined on randomized power-on RAM and that integer POS tracks the exact real-valued path with error ≤ 255/256 px at every frame. The old defect is confirmed on hardware, not just on paper: the pre-commit build measures **1.414 px/f at the h=40 diagonal (−29.3%) vs 2.236 on adjacent headings** — a 58% speed pulse across one 5.6° heading step — while the new build holds 1.75–2.24 px/f across all headings (mean 2.04; segment dither is the integer floor the handoff brief honestly documents). Cadence stays in +1/+1 loop/NMI lockstep for all 104 stepped frames with the ~60-instruction heavier block. All five non-ROTATE ROMs are **byte-identical** pre/post commit. 18/18 + 56/56 + all four gates clean. The handoff Task-1 mechanism checks out against the emulator source with no hardware-model errors. One low finding (suite discrimination), two info notes; none block ship.

## Findings

1. **[low] No test pins the behavior this commit fixes.** `tests/test_split_h_2p_demo.py:336-339` — the rotate cadence test asserts positions merely *change* (`!=` guards); the render-motion test (`test_rot_both_bands_rotate`) runs the **rotfreeze** build, which contains no movement at all. A revert of the LUT scale to integer px, deletion of the fraction strip (`and #$00FF`), or a broken sign-extension branch would pass 18/18 — the exact regression class the owner just reported would be invisible to the suite. Cheap hardening, same pattern as the pointer-binding assert this suite already gained from the previous audit round: inside `test_rot_cadence_true_60fps_in_situ`, read POS/F each step and assert the per-frame delta equals the signed-high-byte decomposition of `move64[h]` (8 `read_u16`s; my probe is a working reference). Does not block ship — the shipped code is verified correct; this is suite discrimination for the future. *(Disposition: remediated in the same commit that lands this report — the cadence gate now carries the exact 8.8 accumulator model, positions AND fractions, both cameras, every step.)*
2. **[info] Handoff Task-1 design: hardware-model VERIFIED SOUND, with one nuance to assert in the spike.** I checked the load-bearing claims against the emulator's DMA controller source (`SnesDmaController.cpp`, upstream): HDMA channels are processed **ascending CH0→CH7** each scanline, so allocating band-2's pair to lower channels and band-1's to higher ones does make band-1's line-0 write land last and win — correct. Non-repeat count-112 semantics confirmed (one transfer at entry load, then silent, next entry at line 112); init also walks channels ascending. Per-channel `$43x7` indirect bank, the `(h & 63)·448 / (h >> 6)` bank math (implies 64-poses-per-bank padded blobs — consistent with "128 poses = 2 banks/blob"), the BANK1..BANKF ROM budget, and the ~1–2% steal estimate (my estimate ~0.6–1%) all check out. **The nuance:** "last write wins" holds for the write-twice matrix registers specifically because a mode-3 unit writes each register's two bytes back-to-back within one channel ($211B,$211B,$211C,$211C), so the shared mode-7 prev-byte write latch can never be split across channels. The spike should assert this (and the allocation-order dependence the brief already flags) so a future transfer-mode change can't silently break the mask.
3. **[info] Residual measured dither, for expectation-setting:** per-4-frame constant-heading segments measure 1.75–2.24 px/f (±12%); per-frame |delta| ∈ {1.00, 1.41, 2.00, 2.24, 2.83}. This is the irreducible integer-register floor of a Bresenham-style accumulator — sub-pixel per frame, exactly the "±0.4 px, invisible at 60 Hz" the handoff brief states — and the *path* speed is exactly 2.0 by construction (proven by the model-exact replay). The remaining visible jerk is the heading snap, correctly deferred to Task 1. (The measured mean per-frame |delta| of 2.04 is a sampling artifact — magnitude of integer deltas is convex — not a speed error.)

**Verified with no findings:**
- **Diff scope:** exactly `asm_repo_staging/templates/split_h_2p_demo/assets/gen_assets.py`, `.../assets/move64.bin`, `.../main.asm`, `docs/sprints/split_h_2p_stress_handoff.md` — nothing else.
- **Decomposition math:** for f ∈ [0,255], v ∈ [−512,512]: s = f+v ∈ [−512,767] (no 16-bit overflow); high byte sign-extended = floor(s/256), low byte = s mod 256, so delta·256 + frac' = f+v exactly. Hand-walk v = $FE96 (−362), f=0: sum $FE96 → high $FE → delta −2, frac 150 (−512+150 = −362 ✓); then 150−362 = $FF2C → −1, frac 44 ✓; then $FEC2 → −2, frac 194 ✓ — 3-frame total −5 px + 194/256, exactly 3·(−362)/256. The code implements precisely this: `xba / and #$00FF / cmp #$0080 / bcc / ora #$FF00` sign-extension, `and #$00FF` strip, and `and #$03FF` wrap is exact for s16 deltas because 1024 | 2^16.
- **LUT:** 64 entries, 64/64 equal to round(−512·sin, −512·cos)(2πh/64); magnitude 511.863–512.445 (within 512±1) at every heading; cardinals exact (0,∓512)/(∓512,0); h=8 = (−362,−362) = $FE96. Delete-all-and-regenerate from `gen_assets.py`: **all 8 assets byte-identical** to committed. Provenance gate clean.
- **Width discipline:** the entire movement block sits in the single-width A16/I16 region; zero `sep`/`rep` inside; the anonymous-label branches join at the same width from both paths; `xba` is width-independent. `make width-check` clean (189 files).
- **WRAM allocation:** F1X–F2Y ($C072–$C079) extend the template-owned scratch run contiguously after PTRTMP; no other template/engine file references those addresses.
- **Non-ROTATE builds:** `move64.bin` incbin and the movement block are both inside `.ifdef ROTATE` — confirmed at the strongest level by **byte-identical** default/freeze/sameorigin/retarget/latch ROMs built from a pre-commit (9c22b79) checkout vs 61580f1.

Handoff Task-1 source citations (emulator DMA controller, upstream `Core/SNES/SnesDmaController.cpp`): HDMA per-scanline processing iterates channels ascending 0→7; non-repeat entry (bit 7 clear) sets `DoTransfer` false after its single transfer until the next entry loads N lines later; frame init walks channels ascending with the first transfers landing in the first per-line HDMA pass — all three exactly as the brief's masking design assumes.
